contract.goal: Ship linear-loom tasks generate <slug> — the load-bearing reconciliation verb. Composes U1's parser + U2's state-fetch + diff with Linear-write mutations. Dry-run by default; --apply applies create/update/rekey; --prune adds safe archives; --prune --force also adds in-flight archives.
acceptance_criteria: cli/lib/apply-diff.ts: applyDiffOps(ops, ctx) walks ops in order create → update → rekey → archive; dispatches projectMilestoneCreate/Update/Archive or issueCreate/Update/Archive per node_kind.
Issue creates set projectMilestoneId for Batches, parentId for Tasks; both set labelIds: [ctx.label.id]. Tree-ordered creates thread parent IDs via composed_key_to_linear_id map.
apply-failed LinearLoomError on any success=false response or missing parent ID; message captures partial-success path.
cli/verbs/tasks.ts: tasksGenerate async verb + TASKS_VERBS map. Default = dry-run; emits {slug, mode, summary, archive_partition, ops, hint}.

--apply applies the non-archive ops + (gated by --prune) safe archives + (gated by --prune --force) in-flight archives. Emits {slug, mode: 'apply', summary, archive_partition, applied, deferred_archives}.
PLAN.md path defaults to projects/<slug>/PLAN.md; --plan-file=<path> overrides.
Wired into VERB_HANDLERS as tasks: TASKS_VERBS.

Production composers: phase title `<slug> · Phase N — <prose>`; batch title `<slug> · Batch <composed_key> — <prose>`; task title bare prose. Body wraps composeLinearDescription with composed-key header.
rules_applied: DESIGN.md § 12.3 (PLAN.md authoritative; create / update / archive paths).
DESIGN.md § 12.7 (idempotency via composed-key matching; resume-from-where-failed via pre-filled composed_key_to_linear_id from existing state).
disqualifiers: Writing to PLAN.md (U4 territory — bidirectional URLs back into PLAN.md).
Silent apply without --apply (dry-run is the safer default).
Marker schema changes (--team-id stays explicit on v1; future polish caches it).
inputs: U2's linear-state.ts (fetchLinearState, composeLinearDescription) + plan-diff.ts (computeDiff, partitionArchiveOps, summarizeDiff, DiffOp shapes)

actions: Wrote cli/lib/apply-diff.ts: ApplyContext interface (snake_case wire-shape mirroring), applyDiffOps walks creates/updates/rekeys/archives in order. Six GraphQL mutation strings: projectMilestoneCreate/Update/Archive + issueCreate/Update/Archive. Tree-ordered create dispatch threads parent linear_ids via composed_key_to_linear_id Map; throws apply-failed on success=false or missing parent.

Wrote cli/verbs/tasks.ts: tasksGenerate parses positional <slug> + --team-id + optional --plan-file/--apply/--prune/--force/--pretty. Reads marker → resolves auth + git → reads + parses PLAN.md → fetches Linear state + label-ID lookup in parallel (Promise.all) → computeDiff with production composeTitleFn + composeBodyFn → emits dry-run summary OR applies the gated op set.

Composers: Phase titles `<slug> · Phase N — <prose>` so they parse cleanly via project-read's existing milestone regex. Batch titles `<slug> · Batch <composed_key> — <prose>` for visual grouping in Linear's flat Issue listing. Task titles bare `<prose>` (Linear nests Sub-Issues under their parent). All bodies wrap composeLinearDescription with composed-key as first parseable line.

Wired tasks namespace into VERB_HANDLERS in cli/linear-loom.ts. CLI dispatch now: tasks → missing-verb with candidates ['generate']; tasks generate → missing-slug; tasks generate <slug> → missing-team-id.

Wrote 20 unit tests: 10 apply-diff (empty list, milestone create, batch under milestone via projectMilestoneId, task under batch via parentId, full phase→batch→task chain in tree order, apply-failed on success=false, apply-failed on missing parent, update sends new title, archive for batch+task vs milestone archive for phase, mixed-op-list order assertion) + 10 tasks verb (missing slug/team-id, marker-unreadable, plan-file-unreadable, dry-run default, label-not-found, --apply applies creates in tree order, --apply without --prune defers archives + lists in deferred_archives, --pretty).

Iteration: addressed one naming-evaluator advisory mid-iteration — unified the JSON output field naming so `archive_partition.safe_to_archive` / `in_flight` matches `deferred_archives.safe_to_archive` / `in_flight` (was inconsistently suffixed _count vs not).
Verified: npm test → 807 passing (was 787 after U2; +20). Panel: all 4 evaluators approved.
corrections: JSON output field rename mid-iteration: archive_partition.safe_to_archive_count → safe_to_archive (and same for in_flight) to match deferred_archives' field names. Resolves a naming-evaluator advisory about within-document field-naming inconsistency.

changes_since_previous: Third unit of Phase 5, stacked on the now-merged U2 (#42). After this PR merges, linear-loom tasks generate ships end-to-end against Linear's API. U4 (final Phase 5 unit) adds the bidirectional cross-reference writes (Linear URLs back into PLAN.md per § 13) so re-runs surface the Linear-side state inside the markdown.
notes_for_pr: Dry-run is the safer default. Operators see what WOULD change before any Linear-side mutation; --apply flips to the action mode. This is a substrate posture, not an operator preference — every linear-loom-side mutation goes through this dry-run-first gate.

--prune (DESIGN.md § 12.4) gates archives. Without --prune, the verb lists archives in deferred_archives but doesn't apply them. With --prune, safe archives (state.type ∈ {backlog, unstarted, undefined}) apply; in-flight stays deferred unless --prune --force is also passed. Three-step ladder: see → archive-safe → archive-anyway.

--team-id is required v1 (Linear's IssueCreate input needs a teamId). Future polish: extend the marker schema to cache the teamId on first project create, defaulting it here. Captured in the PR body's follow-ups list.

Naming-evaluator surfaced two non-blocking observations worth following up later: (a) `Fn` suffix on `resolveAuthFn`/`readFileFn` interface fields is mildly Hungarian (TS already knows they're callable) and the pattern has spread across multiple verb files this session — a small substrate-wide rename in a future PR would unify; (b) ApplyContext uses snake_case (wire-shape) while TasksContext uses camelCase (injected-substrate convention); the boundary at the call site reads loudly but is defensible — worth a one-line comment near ApplyContext's declaration.

parseArgs uses strict: false throughout the verb family, so unknown flags are silently accepted (e.g. typos like `--aply` slip through). Reasonable choice for verbs that may grow flags, but worth surfacing as a future-polish item if operator typo-tolerance becomes a papercut.

Sandbox smoke testing is the validation gate for the Linear API field shapes (the IssueCreateInput field set, the ProjectMilestoneUpdateInput shape, the issueArchive mutation existence). Mocked unit tests cover the request structure + response handling; only a real workspace confirms the live wire.
